doc_title_proposal: Institutional Pitch for WWF, Reserves & Forestry Guards
doc_desc_proposal: WildGuard AI is designed as a low-cost, sustainable ecosystem for NGOs and government agencies. It ensures legal custody of evidence, optimizes patrol coordination with stochastic game theory, and reduces edge camera false alarms by 90%.
prop_desc_1: Fully open-source codebase running on off-the-shelf standard hardware (Arduino/ESP32). No subscription plans, recurring maintenance fees, or proprietary technology vendor lock-ins.
prop_desc_2: All digital evidence gets hashed (SHA-256) on capture and bound to local immutable hash chains. This guarantees data integrity and refutes legal challenges on camera trap photos in court.
prop_desc_3: Edge nodes disable radio modules immediately after transmitting an event (anti-tagging). This prevents radio-equipped poachers from scanning and locating ranger squads or watchtowers.
doc_desc_arduino: The camouflaged, low-cost ground nodes are based on Arduino Nicla Vision or ESP32-CAM. They use a PIR sensor to detect thermal presence/movement, wake the microcontroller from deep-sleep, capture a frame, and execute edge inference via TinyML (TensorFlow Lite for Microcontrollers), filtering out foliage/wind variations before satellite or LoRa transmission.
doc_desc_repos: The open-wildguard-hub framework acts as a central orchestrator, integrating and reusing specific code from the 16 downloaded repositories. Below are the reference directories and how code integrates to implement the planned features:
doc_desc_readiness: The project must remain useful before every subsystem is complete. The first production target is a dependable command-center shell with local persistence, clear fallback behavior, and a documented operational path.
doc_desc_playbooks: These guides describe how the platform should be used in concrete field scenarios. They are written for rangers, operators, and maintainers, not for architecture diagrams.
doc_desc_docs_strategy: `dev.md` is the master technical map. As recurring workflows stabilize, each use case should move into its own focused document so the project can be extended without losing clarity.

readiness_items.title: Offline first
readiness_items.desc: Every critical workflow must continue without network access and sync later when possible.

readiness_items.title: Local state
readiness_items.desc: Events, queues, and evidence must live locally with simple, auditable export paths.

readiness_items.title: Clear fallback
readiness_items.desc: Every automated action needs a degraded-mode behavior that operators can trust.

readiness_items.title: Operator ready
readiness_items.desc: The UI must explain what to do in the field, not just show telemetry.

playbooks.title: Camera trap intrusion
playbooks.trigger: Human or vehicle detected by edge vision.
playbooks.action: Review the event, verify the location, and dispatch the nearest ranger team.
playbooks.output: Incident marked, response routed, snapshot preserved.

playbooks.title: Gunshot response
playbooks.trigger: Acoustic spike consistent with a gunshot or chainsaw.
playbooks.action: Cross-check the sensor cluster and build a rapid response route.
playbooks.output: Localized alert, route plan, evidence hash.

playbooks.title: Offline field report
playbooks.trigger: Ranger returns from patrol without connectivity.
playbooks.action: Capture the report locally and sync it later from base.
playbooks.output: Queued report, preserved timestamps, no data loss.

docs_strategy_items.title: One guide per use case
docs_strategy_items.desc: Split stable workflows into dedicated docs instead of overloading the master guide.

docs_strategy_items.title: Keep master map short
docs_strategy_items.desc: Use dev.md for architecture and conventions only.

docs_strategy_items.title: Write for operators
docs_strategy_items.desc: Each guide should explain steps, failure modes, and expected outputs.

docs_strategy_items.title: Document rollout
docs_strategy_items.desc: Add deployment notes for edge nodes and command centers as separate files.

ideas.title: M1: Modular Hub & API
ideas.desc: Central FastAPI & SQLite API orchestrating physical nodes and OSINT data. Implements store-and-forward logic for offline scenarios.

ideas.title: M2: Edge Vision (CV)
ideas.desc: Image classification on camera traps and drones via YOLOv8. Uses OpenCV edge filters to drop wind/leaf alerts on the microcamera.

ideas.title: M3: Bioacoustics & Triangulation
ideas.desc: Low-power microphone network tracking gunshots and chainsaws. Uses Time Difference of Arrival (TDoA) to calculate shooter coordinates.

ideas.title: M4: 3D Geo-Surveillance
ideas.desc: Georeferenced 3D maps based on GIS, GeoJSON, and PyDeck to highlight patrol pathways and wildlife migration corridors.

ideas.title: M5: Species Intelligence
ideas.desc: Biometric and IoT tracking of collar devices on protected wildlife, triggering real-time geo-fencing alerts.

ideas.title: M6: Offline Field Reporting
ideas.desc: Offline-first mobile application for rangers and communities to log tracks and sightings via periodic base syncs.

ideas.title: M7: OSINT & Cyber-Intelligence
ideas.desc: Ethical scraping of illicit black markets, Telegram, and news across 7 languages. Automatic entities extraction using mDeBERTa NLP.

ideas.title: M8: Prediction & Game Theory
ideas.desc: Integrating PAWS predictive engine and DDPG Reinforcement Learning to generate unpredictable, optimal patrol routes.

ideas.title: M9: Forensic Evidence Vault
ideas.desc: Certifying media evidence using immutable SHA-256 hash chains and auto-generating court chargesheets linked to legal schedules.

ideas.title: M10: Federated AI & Flower
ideas.desc: Collaboratively train YOLOv8 across sanctuaries without sharing raw geolocated images, protecting wildlife data sovereignty.

osint.title: Ivory Tusk Seizure at Park Borders
osint.desc: Forest police stopped a van carrying 4 raw ivory tusks prepared for cross-border smuggling. Two arrests.

osint.title: Online Exotic Wildlife Black Market Busted
osint.desc: Detected private Telegram channel dedicated to selling rare reptiles and protected songbirds sourced from Chitwan sanctuary.

alert_dispatch: Quick response patrol dispatched to sensor:
alert_route_calculated: Minimum Spanning Tree (Kruskal MST) patrol route generated connecting all forest stations.
chargesheet_instructions: Select an OSINT event to draft the legal chargesheet...
